# Bubbles v5.2 — Flip Advisory → Primary

> Status: DESIGN. Not implemented.
> Builds on: v5.1.0 (M1–M9 shipped, all advisory/additive).
> Target: ship before v6 MCP migration begins.
> Constraint: anti-fabrication monotonically stronger; no operator capability removed; no mode rename; no agent contract change.

---

## Theme

v5.1 introduced typed plumbing (tool-log, evidence-bridge, diff-evidence-guard, gate-meta facade, code-search facade, schema validators) as **opt-in / advisory** so the framework + 5 downstream repos could absorb the change without breakage. v5.2 flips the bridges to **primary** so the typed paths actually do enforcement, and consolidates the gate registry so v6 MCP work has a single source to point at.

This release is small on net surface (some advisory paths get deleted) but strictly stronger on anti-fabrication.

---

## Non-Negotiable Invariants

1. Anti-fabrication monotonically stronger or equal.
2. No gate is removed; gates may be consolidated/generated/moved only.
3. Markdown evidence path stays valid for the entire v5.2 cycle (operator UX unchanged).
4. Every downstream repo's existing `report.md` files keep validating without rewrite.
5. No new operator command added; no command removed.
6. Pure mechanical upgrade via `install.sh --local-source`.

---

## Work Items

| # | Item | Files | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Tool-log becomes primary evidence path with grandfather | `bubbles/scripts/evidence-tool-log-bridge.sh`, `bubbles/scripts/guards/evidence-depth.sh` (or `state-transition-guard.sh` Check 9), `bubbles/schemas/tool-call.schema.json` v=2 | Gate accepts EITHER (a) tool-log entry whose `cmd` matches the DoD's recorded command AND whose `ts` is within the spec's `lastActiveAt` window, OR (b) traditional ≥10-line raw markdown evidence. Both paths preserved. Tool-log path emits a green check; markdown-only path emits an advisory note. |
| F2 | Diff-evidence-guard goes default-on for new DoD items | `bubbles/scripts/diff-evidence-guard.sh`, `bubbles/scripts/state-transition-guard.sh` | Guard runs on every spec where `state.json.modernization.diffEvidence == "enforce"` OR spec was created after `2026-06-04`. For older specs the guard runs in `--advisory` mode (warn only). Operator opt-in flag `diffEvidence: enforce` in `state.json` is documented. |
| F3 | Schema-version `tool-call.jsonl` | `bubbles/schemas/tool-call.schema.json` → v2 (adds `schemaVersion`, `sessionId`, `framework`, `agent`); `bubbles/scripts/tool-log.sh` writes `schemaVersion=2`; reader accepts v1 + v2 | Old v1 logs continue to validate. New writes are v2. Migration is forward-only; no rewrite of historical logs. |
| F4 | Gate registry consolidation | New `bubbles/registry/gates.yaml` (canonical); `bubbles/workflows.yaml` `gates:` block becomes generated by `bubbles/scripts/generate-gates-block.sh`; `gate-meta.sh` reads only from registry | Single file owns every gate id, name, description, severity, owner agent, status (`active`/`deprecated`). Workflows.yaml `gates:` is byte-identical before/after generation on first commit. `framework-validate` fails if the generated block diverges from the committed copy. |
| F5 | Result-envelope JSON: validator runs in framework-validate | `bubbles/scripts/result-envelope-validate.sh`, `bubbles/scripts/framework-validate.sh` | `framework-validate` greps every agent's `agent.md` for a `result_envelope:` fenced JSON block AND validates each against `result-envelope.schema.json`. Missing block is advisory in v5.2 (warn); becomes blocking in v6. |
| F6 | Code-search facade auto-selects on first call | `bubbles/scripts/code-search.sh` | On first invocation detects `rg` → uses it; falls back to `grep`. Logs the choice once to `.specify/runtime/code-search.tool` so subsequent calls don't re-detect. No interface change. |
| F7 | Model-tier advisory now emits a structured warning | `bubbles/scripts/model-tier-advisory.sh`, workflow agent invocation | When mode's `modelFloor:` is set and host model self-reports below floor, the advisory now writes a `model-tier-warning` line to `.specify/runtime/tool-calls.jsonl` so the warning is durable + auditable. UX text unchanged. |
| F8 | Selftests for F1–F7 | `bubbles/scripts/*-selftest.sh` (extend existing; add new ones for F2 grandfather rule and F4 generator round-trip) | Each F item has a selftest. `framework-validate` runs all of them. |
| F9 | CHANGELOG + release | `CHANGELOG.md`, `VERSION` → `5.2.0`, tag, `release-check` exit 0 | Tagged on `main`. Downstream upgrade recipe is `install.sh --local-source` with no manual steps. |

---

## Backward Compatibility Matrix

| Existing artifact | v5.2 behavior |
|---|---|
| `report.md` with markdown-only evidence | Still validates (path b in F1). |
| `tool-call.jsonl` v1 entries | Still validates. New writes are v2. |
| Existing spec without `diffEvidence: enforce` | Diff-evidence-guard runs advisory only. |
| Existing agent without `result_envelope:` block | Warn-only in v5.2; blocking in v6. |
| Downstream repo without `bubbles/registry/gates.yaml` | `install.sh --local-source` creates it; existing workflows.yaml `gates:` block becomes generated. |

---

## Migration Steps (per downstream repo)

1. `install.sh --local-source` (mechanical; copies new scripts + schemas + registry).
2. Run `bash .github/bubbles/scripts/framework-validate.sh` — must exit 0.
3. Optional: opt a spec into strict diff-evidence by setting `state.json.modernization.diffEvidence = "enforce"`.
4. Optional: start emitting `tool-call.jsonl` v2 entries — older specs and tools keep working.
5. Push (existing pre-push hook reruns full validation).

---

## What v5.2 Does Not Do

- Does not remove the markdown evidence path.
- Does not delete any v5.1 advisory script (they become primary, not gone).
- Does not introduce MCP.
- Does not change agent prompts or mode names.
- Does not require any operator to rewrite historical `report.md` files.

---

## Selftest Plan

| Selftest | Asserts |
|---|---|
| `evidence-tool-log-bridge-selftest.sh` | Spec with tool-log entry passes evidence gate even with empty markdown fences. Spec without tool-log entry still passes with ≥10-line markdown. Spec with neither fails. |
| `diff-evidence-guard-selftest.sh` (extend) | Spec with `diffEvidence: enforce` and a DoD item claiming "file X created" but no diff entry → guard exit 1. Spec without flag → exit 0 with warning. |
| `tool-log-selftest.sh` (extend) | v1 entries read OK. New writes have `schemaVersion: 2`. Mixed v1+v2 log validates. |
| `gates-registry-selftest.sh` (new) | Round-trip: registry → generated `gates:` block byte-identical to committed copy. Renaming a gate ID in registry without regenerating fails the test. |
| `result-envelope-validate-selftest.sh` (extend) | Agent file with valid envelope passes. Agent file with malformed envelope warns (does not fail) in v5.2. |
| `code-search-selftest.sh` (extend) | `rg` available → picked; `rg` removed from PATH → falls back to `grep`. |
| `model-tier-advisory-selftest.sh` (extend) | Floor=`claude-sonnet-4`, reported=`claude-haiku-3` → warning written to tool-log. Floor=any, reported=above → no warning. |

---

## Risk Register

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Downstream repo has thousands of historical `report.md` files | F1 keeps markdown path; F2 default-off for old specs. |
| Tool-log v1→v2 breaks an existing reader | Reader accepts both versions; CHANGELOG calls out schemaVersion field. |
| Generated `gates:` block diverges from committed in a downstream repo | F4 selftest catches it; install.sh regenerates on apply. |
| Operator confused by "warn" vs "block" | Single doc page (`docs/v5.2-evidence-modes.md`) describing the 3 modes (advisory/grandfather/enforce). |

---

## Open Questions

1. Should F2 grandfather cutoff be a date (`2026-06-04`) or a version (`introducedAtFramework: 5.2`)? Recommendation: date — simpler to reason about.
2. Does `result-envelope-validate` become blocking in v5.2 or v6? Plan says v6; revisit if v5.2 sees zero envelope failures across all 5 downstreams.
3. Do we ship `bubbles/registry/gates.yaml` empty in v5.2 (operator opts in) or pre-populated with all 100+ gates from `workflows.yaml`? Recommendation: pre-populated, F4 generator round-trips it.
